summary: A stellar Vegas night captured on a sound‑board tape – dazzling Help>Slip>Frank, soaring So Many Roads and an unforgettable Attics of My Life, with generally good audio despite occasional mix hiccups.
review: The performance is widely celebrated for its late‑night magic: the opening Help → Slipknot → Franklin’s sets a high bar, Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain flows into a transcendent Attics of My Life, and So Many Roads erupts with Jerry’s screaming lead.  Most listeners praise the second set and the guest appearance by Steve Miller, while the soundboard source delivers clear overall sound; a few reviewers note that Jerry’s guitar drops out early in Slipknot and there is occasional popping or hiss on the left channel.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most rating 4‑5 stars, repeatedly highlight exceptional musical moments and only minor, isolated recording flaws – enough to keep the overall experience very strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear SBD with crisp instrument separation.  Reported issues: early‑Slipknot guitar low/missing, occasional left‑channel popping during Attics, faint hiss and a few drop‑outs.  No major distortion.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
Spoonful > The Other One > Morning Dew
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers love the night‑time arc: a strong first set capped by Help>Slip>Frank, an explosive second set that builds through Scarlet and Fire into Attics, then a memorable encore of Baba O'Riley → Tomorrow Never Knows.  The guest spot by Steve Miller is noted as a fun extra but not essential to the flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring solos on So Many Roads and powerful leads on Attics; however several note his guitar disappearing early in Slipknot and being low in the mix overall.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for a “crazy” bass run on The Other One and generally solid support throughout; one reviewer mentions him shushing Steve Miller toward the end of Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive nods to his vocal delivery on Queen Jane Approximately and his rhythm work, plus occasional Pete Townshend‑style wind‑mill impressions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboardist) receives specific praise for inventive solos and overall solid playing; post‑Brent era comments note the keys sound good in this large outdoor venue.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming and space segments are described as “high‑tek”, energetic, and a highlight of the show, though one reviewer mentions the new in‑ear system causing occasional sedation.
